title: Walsh vetoes more than half of Syracuse lawmakers' budget amendments
summary: Mayor Walsh vetoed 45 of 82 budget amendments (55%) that the Common Council passed, cutting $16M from his $348M spending plan. Key vetoes targeted public safety (police/fire), revenue-generating departments (Code Enforcement, Administrative Adjudication), and eliminated a proposed property tax increase while preserving a water rate increase. The Council has two weeks to override vetoes with a six-vote majority.

political_signal.description: Executive-legislative branch conflict over budget priorities. Mayor prioritizes public safety and revenue generation; Council prioritizes spending cuts. Veto overrides possible but uncertain based on stated willingness to compromise by some members.

processing_notes: Timeline: Two-week deadline for final budget approval; veto override requires 6 votes
Public safety was primary contention area—all 45 vetoes included public safety-related amendments
Unanimous initial vote on amendments suggests potential Council cohesion, but override prospects unclear
Revenue generation framed as strategic priority by Mayor; may signal fiscal stress or sustainability concerns
